steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The notes present a broad category claim and then back it with a specific example (Grenada's nutmeg, Mexico's eagle). The student must find the choice that mirrors that structure: state a category of flag symbols and support it with a concrete example from the notes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Grenada's flag includes a clove of nutmeg, a symbol that is important to that country's national identity.": Narrows to one country and makes unsupported claims about national importance. No category, no structural match. ✗
  • "Vexillology is the study of flags: accordingly, vexillologists are interested in flags from around the world.": Abandons the topic of symbols entirely to discuss the study of flags. Off-topic. ✗
  • "The flags of some countries include symbols of plants: Grenada's, for example, includes a clove of nutmeg.": States a category (plant symbols) and immediately supports it with Grenada's nutmeg from the notes. Matches the required structure. ✓
  • "Many countries feature symbols on their flags, and the study of these designs is known as vexillology.": Announces a category but provides no supporting example. Incomplete. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
